title: Vendor Payout Exception Recovery
description: A payments-and-settlement workflow that controls release, routing, and execution timing across multiple rails and liquidity constraints. Focus case: vendor payout exception recovery.
assumptions: Ledger and rail availability flags in the model accurately reflect allocatable resources at runtime.
Compliance credentials and third-party receipts are authoritative and measurable by the system.
Routing tables and execution profiles are up to date and contain valid fallbacks.
Monotonic state transitions: once a resource is reserved, it remains reserved until an explicit release action.
risks: Liquidity shortfall: reserved resources may be insufficient at execution time causing failed settlement.
Routing failure or stale execution profile leading to delayed or misrouted payouts.
Duplicate execution risk if confirmations or receipts are delayed or misapplied.
Regulatory non-compliance if incorrect credentials or compliance artifacts are accepted.
